attacking_style: Attacks with 2km remaining on final climbs when in form, targeting time bonuses and marginal GC gains rather than stage wins. Vulnerable on descents at high speed, particularly when trying to follow elite descenders.
team_tactics: When protected as leader, relies on tempo-setting domestiques to isolate GC rivals into stalemate situations rather than explosive attacks. Will sacrifice personal ambitions for team jersey defense (e.g., working for Van Avermaet's yellow).
terrain_preference: Dominant in time trials (23-34km range), consistently taking 30+ seconds from GC rivals. Excels on HC summit finishes when fresh but struggles when isolated without teammates. Crosswind/echelon positioning is a repeated weakness.
vulnerability: Catastrophic sensitivity to illness/crashes derailing entire campaigns (6+ DNF/DNS observations). Positioning errors in echelons/splits cost 1-3 minutes on flat stages despite GC contention. Descending at race-winning pace exposes crash risk.
key_patterns: ITT specialist: multiple wins with 12-37 second margins, consistently beats GC rivals in 23-34km efforts
Echelon vulnerability: caught in splits at Paris-Nice 2020, Giro 2022, Tour 2019, loses 1+ minutes
Health fragility: gastroenteritis, illness, crashes force 6+ race abandonments across all seasons analyzed
Late-career resurgence 2020-21: podium at Tour age 35, Dauphiné win, Catalunya 2nd shows sustained form
2km attack signature: repeatedly launches decisive moves exactly 2km from finish on climbs (Dauphiné 2017, bonuses 2021)
Conservative descending: deliberately cautious cornering costs time versus Froome, crashes when pushing limits
form_trajectory: negative: 2022 shows decline with DNF from illness, caught in echelon splits, relegated to pure domestique role compared to 2020-21 podium/win trajectory
